method: ACTION-BED is a task-driven Bayesian Experimental Design (BED) framework that reformulates experimental design in terms of an expected future loss (EFL) on downstream actions, moving away from traditional expected uncertainty reductions (EUR).
It transforms EFLs into singly intractable objectives that can be jointly optimized with respect to both a design policy and a downstream action policy using stochastic gradients.

The method is naturally implicit, requiring only the ability to sample from the joint model over model parameters and data, and evaluate a differentiable downstream loss function, thus sidestepping the need for explicit posterior or marginal likelihood estimation.

ACTION-BED avoids the computational challenges associated with doubly intractable objectives found in traditional EUR-based methods, allowing for more effective, efficient, and simpler learning of design policies.
It provides easy customization to different downstream tasks and losses, addressing the difficulty of tailoring generic uncertainty measures (like Shannon entropy) to specific tasks.
